evals.prompt: Here is the anatomy I want for a manual test case: title, preconditions, steps, expected results, test data. Take this acceptance criterion and write one well-formed manual test case from it: 'Given a logged-out user, when they submit the login form with a valid email and a wrong password 5 times, then the account is locked for 15 minutes.' Show the full structure.
expected_patterns: one assertion per step OR single expected result

evals.prompt: Bulk-generate manual test cases from this user story for TestRail and give me the API payloads to create them. Story: 'As a shopper I can apply a discount code at checkout; valid codes reduce the total, expired codes show an error, already-used codes are rejected.' Each case needs separated steps.
anti_patterns: custom_steps (single text field for separated steps)
basic auth with username and password only
one giant case for all three rules

evals.prompt: We use Xray on Jira Cloud. I need to import 12 manual test cases programmatically and also a couple of Gherkin scenarios. What API should I use and what does the request look like? Show authentication and the test type fields.
anti_patterns: username/password basic auth for Xray Cloud

evals.prompt: Lint this test suite for ambiguous steps that aren't deterministically verifiable and rewrite the bad ones. Steps: 1) Open the app. Expected: app opens. 2) Click around the dashboard. Expected: everything works. 3) Enter some data. Expected: it looks right. 4) Wait a bit. Expected: the report is ready.
expected_patterns: ambiguous OR not verifiable OR non-deterministic
everything works OR it looks right (flagged)
click around OR some data OR wait a bit (flagged)
specific element OR exact value OR explicit selector
anti_patterns: leaving 'everything works' as a valid expected result
approving 'wait a bit' without a concrete condition

evals.prompt: Produce a CSV I can import into TestRail for a 'Password Reset' section with three cases, mapping columns to TestRail fields so the importer recognizes title, steps, and expected results without manual remapping.
anti_patterns: free-form prose with no header row
single Description column for steps and expected
JSON instead of CSV when CSV was requested

evals.prompt: We use Qase. Give me the API call to create three test cases in bulk under suite 'Checkout', each with structured steps (action, expected result, input data). Include auth header and the suite reference.
anti_patterns: one POST per case when bulk endpoint exists

evals.prompt: How should I organize a growing TestRail repository for a web + mobile product so it stays navigable, and how do suites vs sections vs the single-repository mode change that decision?
expected_patterns: single repository OR single suite mode OR multiple suites
shallow hierarchy OR avoid deep nesting
anti_patterns: put every test in the root section
duplicate the same case across suites

evals.prompt: Set up traceability between requirements and test cases. We have Xray on Jira and stories with Jira keys. How do I link cases to requirements and get a coverage report that shows which stories have no tests?
expected_patterns: tests OR test issue link OR coverage
uncovered OR no tests OR gaps
Jira issue link OR issue key
anti_patterns: manual spreadsheet as the only traceability mechanism
link tests to test executions instead of requirements

evals.prompt: Give me criteria for when a manual test case should graduate to automation, and when it should stay manual. We have a backlog of 400 manual cases in Zephyr Scale and limited automation capacity.
expected_patterns: high frequency OR run often OR every release
stable OR low churn OR deterministic
exploratory OR one-off OR rarely run (stay manual)
ROI OR cost.*benefit OR maintenance cost
anti_patterns: automate flaky or frequently changing UI first

evals.prompt: We use Zephyr Scale on Jira Cloud. Write the REST call to create a manual test case with named steps in folder 'Smoke', including authentication. Then show how to add the test steps.
anti_patterns: steps embedded in the create-case body as a single text blob
